patch.value:
## Context

File: src/cli/commands/telemetry.ts lines 199-253 — buildTelemetryStatsBuckets groups events; only count and max_attempts are computed. command_finish events contain payload.duration_ms (set in buildCommandFinishPayload, runtime.ts:924).

File: src/core/telemetry/runtime.ts:924 — duration_ms is always present in command_finish payloads at all capture levels.

The queue inspector in telemetry.ts already reads the full event JSON; adding duration_ms extraction and a simple percentile sort costs a single pass over the existing entries array.

## Acceptance criteria

- Each TelemetryStatsBucket gains optional fields: duration_p50_ms, duration_p95_ms, duration_max_ms (present only when command_finish events exist for that bucket).
- Computed from command_finish events only; command_start / command_error events are excluded.
- Output of pm telemetry stats includes the new fields when present.
- Unit tests cover: empty bucket (fields absent), single entry, multi-entry percentile correctness.

## Evidence

src/cli/commands/telemetry.ts:199 — bucket shape, no duration fields.
src/core/telemetry/runtime.ts:924 — duration_ms in command_finish payload.
Surface duration_ms percentile breakdown in pm telemetry stats (p50/p95 per command)

pm telemetry stats groups queued events by command and reports count and max_attempts, but does not surface latency distribution (duration_ms is present in command_finish payloads). Without percentile data in the local queue, the only way to detect p95 regressions is by querying Sentry or the remote stack. Adding p50/p95/max duration_ms to telemetry stats gives an always-available, zero-network performance signal for the most recent queued window.
pm telemetry stats output includes duration_p50_ms, duration_p95_ms, duration_max_ms per command bucket when finish events are in the queue; tests assert correct percentile math.

- Each TelemetryStatsBucket gains optional fields: duration_p50_ms, duration_p95_ms, duration_max_ms (present only when command_finish events exist for that bucket).
- Computed from command_finish events only; command_start / command_error events are excluded.
- Output of pm telemetry stats includes the new fields when present.
- Unit tests cover: empty bucket (fields absent), single entry, multi-entry percentile correctness.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Implemented in PR (branch telemetry-local-latency-error-agent-stats) bundled with pm-gsoe (same buildTelemetryStatsBuckets pass) and pm-fbyu (agent-identity dimension) under epic pm-5oj5. Nearest-rank p50/p95 + max over command_finish duration_ms; verified end-to-end in a temp PM_GLOBAL_PATH sandbox and via unit tests. Full coverage 100/100/100/100 (4551 tests), static gate green.

patch.value: pm telemetry stats now reports per-command latency percentiles (duration_p50_ms/p95_ms/max_ms, nearest-rank) computed from command_finish payloads; start/error events excluded; fields absent when no finite duration present.
Shipped local latency percentiles in telemetry stats with full unit coverage; 100/100/100/100.
pm telemetry stats surfaces p50/p95/max duration per command bucket from queued command_finish events with no network call.
Nearest-rank percentiles emitted only when a finite duration_ms exists; verified in sandbox + 4 new unit cases; 100/100/100/100.
